response.justification: Bogdan has a broad software engineering background with solid distributed systems exposure and strong testing discipline, but critically fails to meet the most heavily weighted minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ experience. His C++ usage appears limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, with C++ not even listed among his current skills. The role also requires 5 years of large-scale infrastructure/compute platform work, whereas his infrastructure exposure is largely incidental (Kubernetes for a side project, AWS CDK for pricing tooling) rather than the core focus of his roles. He has never held a formal technical leadership title, and his cloud architecture depth is shallow relative to what an internal cloud compute platform demands.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency D-lang services
Deep, consistent commitment to software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community knowledge-sharing across many roles
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, D, Java, C#, Node, embedded) showing adaptability and systems-level thinking beyond a single stack
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) against a hard requirement of 8 years — the single largest disqualifier
No formal technical leadership role; staff-level titles suggest individual-contributor seniority, not team or organizational technical direction
Cloud compute platform experience is shallow and peripheral: Kubernetes appears only in a personal open-source project, and AWS CDK was used for pricing logic, not infrastructure strategy
